segments.text: He also said to his disciples,
There was a certain rich man who had a manager. An accusation was made to him that this man was wasting his possessions.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He called him, and said to him, ‘What is this that I hear about you? Give an accounting of your management, for you can no longer be manager.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “The manager said within himself, ‘What will I do, seeing that my lord is taking away the management position from me? I don’t have strength to dig. I am ashamed to beg.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I know what I will do, so that when I am removed from management, they may receive me into their houses.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Calling each one of his lord’s debtors to him, he said to the first, ‘How much do you owe to my lord?’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He said, ‘A hundred batos of oil.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill, and sit down quickly and write fifty.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then he said to another, ‘How much do you owe?’ He said, ‘A hundred cors of wheat.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill, and write eighty.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “His lord commended the dishonest manager because he had done wisely, for the children of this world are, in their own generation, wiser than the children of the light.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I tell you, make for yourselves friends by means of unrighteous mammon, so that when you fail, they may receive you into the eternal tents.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He who is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much. He who is dishonest in a very little is also dishonest in much.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If therefore you have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you have not been faithful in that which is another’s, who will give you that which is your own?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: No servant can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one and love the other; or else he will hold to one and despise the other. You aren’t able to serve God and Mammon.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Pharisees, who were lovers of money, also heard all these things, and they scoffed at him.
He said to them,
You are those who justify yourselves in the sight of men, but God knows your hearts. For that which is exalted among men is an abomination in the sight of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “The law and the prophets were until John. From that time the Good News of God’s Kingdom is preached, and everyone is forcing his way into it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for one tiny stroke of a pen in the law to fall.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Everyone who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery. He who marries one who is divorced from a husband commits adultery.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Now there was a certain rich man, and he was clothed in purple and fine linen, living in luxury every day.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: A certain beggar, named Lazarus, was taken to his gate, full of sores,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and desiring to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man’s table. Yes, even the dogs came and licked his sores.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The beggar died, and he was carried away by the angels to Abraham’s bosom. The rich man also died and was buried.
segments.speaker: An Angel of the LORD

segments.text: In Hades,  he lifted up his eyes, being in torment, and saw Abraham far off, and Lazarus at his bosom.
segments.speaker: The rich man

segments.text: He cried and said, ‘Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue! For I am in anguish in this flame.’
segments.speaker: The rich man

segments.text: “But Abraham said, ‘Son, remember that you, in your lifetime, received your good things, and Lazarus, in the same way, bad things. But here he is now comforted and you are in anguish.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Besides all this, between us and you there is a great gulf fixed, that those who want to pass from here to you are not able, and that no one may cross over from there to us.’
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: “He said, ‘I ask you therefore, father, that you would send him to my father’s house—
segments.speaker: The rich man

segments.text: for I have five brothers—that he may testify to them, so they won’t also come into this place of torment.’
segments.speaker: The rich man

segments.text: “But Abraham said to him, ‘They have Moses and the prophets. Let them listen to them.’
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: “He said, ‘No, father Abraham, but if one goes to them from the dead, they will repent.’
segments.speaker: The rich man

segments.text: “He said to him, ‘If they don’t listen to Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded if one rises from the dead.’
segments.speaker: Abraham
